0.ai_notes: 'diabetic': a person who has the medical condition diabetes, which affects how the body processes sugar.
0.remarks: 'diabetic': 患有糖尿病的人，该病影响身体处理糖分的方式。

1.original: Right after I discovered I had this ailment, about 5,000 hypodermics ago,
1.b1: Shortly after I found out I had this illness, about 5,000 injections ago,
1.cn: 在我刚发现自己患有这种疾病后，大约是在五千次注射前，
1.ai_notes:
'ailment': a sickness or illness, usually not very serious but often long-lasting.
'hypodermics': medical needles used for injections.
1.remarks:
'ailment': 小病或疾病，通常不太严重但往往持续时间较长。
'hypodermics': 用于注射的医用针头。

2.original: I was warned, Diabetes is a physical condition, but the biggest damage results from having a negative attitude about it.
2.b1: I was warned that diabetes is a physical problem, but the worst damage comes from having a negative attitude.
2.cn: 我被告知，糖尿病是一种生理疾病，但最大的伤害来自于对它持有的消极态度。
2.ai_notes:
'physical condition': a state of the body, often used to describe a medical health issue.
'results from': happens because of something else.
2.remarks:
'physical condition': 身体状态，常用于描述健康问题。
'results from': 由……引起；起因于……

3.original: Worry about it, and you may have real trouble.
3.b1: If you worry about it, you might have real problems.
3.cn: 如果你为此忧心忡忡，你可能会遇到真正的麻烦。
3.ai_notes: 'real trouble': serious problems or difficult situations.
3.remarks: 'real trouble': 严重的问题或困难的处境。

4.original: Naturally, since the discovery of my own diabetes, I've gotten to know a great many other diabetics.
4.b1: Since I discovered I had diabetes, I have met many other people with the same condition.
4.cn: 自然地，自从发现自己患有糖尿病以来，我结识了许多其他糖尿病患者。
4.ai_notes: 'a great many': a large number of; many.

5.original: Let me tell you about two extremes.
5.b1: Let me tell you about two very different examples.
5.cn: 让我来告诉你两个极端的例子。
5.ai_notes: 'extremes': two opposite ends of a range; here, it refers to two very different ways of handling a problem.
5.remarks: 'extremes': 范围的两端；此处指处理问题的两种截然不同的方式。

6.original: One fellow who has a very mild case belongs to that fraternity of the living dead.
6.b1: One man has a very mild case, but he acts like he is already dead.
6.cn: 有一个病情很轻的人，却活得像个行尸走肉。
6.ai_notes:
'fellow': a friendly or informal word for a man.
'fraternity of the living dead': a metaphorical way to describe people who are physically alive but act as if they have given up on life.
6.remarks:
'fellow': 对男性的友好或非正式称呼。
'fraternity of the living dead': 比喻那些身体活着但表现得像已放弃生活的人。

7.original: Obsessed with the fear of the weather, he is usually ridiculously bundled up.
7.b1: He is so afraid of the weather that he always wears too many clothes.
7.cn: 他因为害怕天气变化，总是把自己裹得严严实实。
7.ai_notes:
'obsessed with': thinking about something so much that it becomes unhealthy or difficult to stop.
'bundled up': wearing many layers of warm clothing.
7.remarks:
'obsessed with': 对某事过度关注，以至于变得不健康或难以停止。
'bundled up': 穿上多层保暖衣物。

8.original: He's afraid of infection, so he shuns anybody who has the slightest sniffle.
8.b1: He is afraid of getting sick, so he avoids anyone who has a cold.
8.cn: 他害怕被传染，所以避开任何有一点感冒症状的人。
8.ai_notes:
'shuns': avoids someone or something on purpose.
'sniffle': the sound made when someone has a runny nose from a cold; it implies a minor illness.
8.remarks:
'shuns': 故意避开某人或某事。
'sniffle': 因感冒流鼻涕发出的声音；暗示轻微的疾病。

9.original: He's afraid of overexertion, so he does almost nothing.
9.b1: He is afraid of working too hard, so he does almost nothing.
9.cn: 他害怕过度劳累，所以几乎什么都不做。
9.ai_notes: 'overexertion': doing too much physical activity or working too hard.
9.remarks: 'overexertion': 过度体力活动或工作过累。

10.original: He spends most of his mental energy worrying about what might happen.
10.b1: He spends most of his time worrying about what might go wrong.
10.cn: 他把大部分精力都花在担心可能发生的事情上。
10.ai_notes: 'mental energy': the focus and thought power used to deal with daily life.
10.remarks: 'mental energy': 处理日常生活所需的专注力和思考能力。

11.original: He bores other people, telling them how awful his problem really is.
11.b1: He bores others by complaining about how terrible his problem is.
11.cn: 他总是向别人抱怨自己的问题有多严重，让人感到厌烦。
11.ai_notes: 'bores': makes someone feel tired or uninterested because of constant talking.
11.remarks: 'bores': 因不停说话而使人感到厌烦或无趣。

12.original: His real ailment is not diabetes.
12.b1: His real problem is not diabetes.
12.cn: 他真正的问题不是糖尿病。
12.ai_notes: 'ailment': a synonym for illness or a persistent health problem.
12.remarks: 'ailment': 疾病或持续性健康问题的同义词。

13.original: Rather, he's a victim of health excusitis.
13.b1: Instead, he is a victim of using his health as an excuse.
13.cn: 相反，他是“健康借口症”的受害者。
13.ai_notes: 'excusitis': a humorous, made-up word combining 'excuse' and '-itis' (a suffix for inflammation/disease); it means the habit of constantly making excuses.
13.remarks: 'excusitis': 一个幽默的自造词，结合了“excuse”（借口）和“-itis”（炎症/疾病后缀）；意指不断找借口的习惯。

14.original: He has pitied himself into being an invalid.
14.b1: He feels so sorry for himself that he has become an invalid.
14.cn: 他因为过度自怜，把自己变成了一个废人。
14.ai_notes:
'pitied himself into': allowed his own self-pity to cause him to become something.
'invalid': a person who is weak or disabled and cannot care for themselves.
14.remarks:
'pitied himself into': 因自我怜悯而导致自己变成某种状态。
'invalid': 体弱或残疾、无法自理的人。

15.original: The other extreme is a division manager for a large publishing company.
15.b1: The other extreme is a manager at a large publishing company.
15.cn: 另一个极端是一位大型出版公司的部门经理。
15.ai_notes: 'division manager': a person in charge of a specific department or section within a large company.
15.remarks: 'division manager': 大公司中负责特定部门或分部的人。

16.b1: He has a serious case of diabetes.
16.ai_notes: 'severe case': a serious or advanced level of a disease.
16.remarks: 'severe case': 疾病的严重或晚期阶段。

17.original: He takes about thirty times as much insulin as the fellow just mentioned.
17.b1: He takes much more insulin than the first man.
17.cn: 他注射的胰岛素量是刚才提到的那个人的三十倍。
17.ai_notes: 'insulin': a hormone used by diabetics to control blood sugar levels.
17.remarks: 'insulin': 糖尿病患者用于控制血糖水平的激素。

18.original: But he is not living to be sick.
18.b1: But he does not live just to be sick.
18.cn: 但他活着不是为了生病。
18.ai_notes: 'living to be sick': a phrase meaning that someone's entire life is focused on their illness.
18.remarks: 'living to be sick': 指某人的整个生活都围绕着疾病转。

19.original: He is living to enjoy his work and have fun.
19.b1: He lives to enjoy his work and have fun.
19.cn: 他活着是为了享受工作和生活。
19.ai_notes: 'have fun': to enjoy oneself and experience happiness.
19.remarks: 'have fun': 享受乐趣并体验快乐。

20.original: One day he said to me, Sure, it is an inconvenience, but so is shaving.
20.b1: One day he told me, "It is annoying, but so is shaving."
20.cn: 有一天他对我讲：“确实，这很不方便，但刮胡子也不方便啊。”
20.ai_notes: 'inconvenience': something that causes minor trouble or annoyance.
20.remarks: 'inconvenience': 造成轻微麻烦或烦恼的事物。

21.original: But I'm not going to think myself to bed.
21.b1: I will not let my thoughts make me stay in bed.
21.cn: 但我绝不会因为自己的想法而卧床不起。
21.ai_notes: 'think myself to bed': a creative way of saying 'letting my negative thoughts make me feel so sick that I have to stay in bed'.
21.remarks: 'think myself to bed': 一种创造性的说法，意为“让消极想法使自己感到生病以至于必须卧床”。

22.original: When I take those shots, I just praise the guys who discovered insulin.
22.b1: When I take my medicine, I just thank the people who discovered insulin.
22.cn: 当我注射胰岛素时，我只会感激那些发现它的人。
22.ai_notes: 'praise': to express admiration or thanks for someone's achievements.
22.remarks: 'praise': 对某人的成就表示赞赏或感谢。

23.original: A good friend of mine, a widely known college educator, came home from Europe in 1945, minus one arm.
23.b1: A good friend of mine, a famous teacher, returned from Europe in 1945 after losing an arm.
23.cn: 我的一位好朋友，一位知名的大学教育家，1945年从欧洲回国时失去了一条手臂。
23.ai_notes: 'minus': in this context, it means 'without' or 'having lost'.
23.remarks: 'minus': 在此语境下，意为“没有”或“失去”。

24.original: Despite his handicap, John is always smiling, always helping others.
24.b1: Even with his disability, John is always smiling and helping others.
24.cn: 尽管有残疾，约翰总是面带微笑，乐于助人。
24.ai_notes: 'handicap': a physical or mental disability that makes certain tasks more difficult.
24.remarks: 'handicap': 使某些任务变得更困难的身体或精神残疾。

25.original: He's about as optimistic as anyone I know.
25.b1: He is the most optimistic person I know.
25.cn: 他是我认识的人中最乐观的。
25.ai_notes: 'optimistic': feeling positive and hopeful about the future.
25.remarks: 'optimistic': 对未来感到积极和充满希望。

26.original: One day, he and I had a long talk about his handicap.
26.b1: One day, we had a long talk about his disability.
26.cn: 有一天，我们长谈了一次，聊到了他的残疾。
26.ai_notes: 'long talk': a deep, serious, or extended conversation.
26.remarks: 'long talk': 一次深入、严肃或延长的谈话。

27.original: It's just an arm," he said.
27.b1: "It is just an arm," he said.
27.ai_notes: 'It's just an arm': a way of minimizing a loss to show that it does not define the person's whole life.
27.remarks: 'It's just an arm': 一种将损失最小化的说法，表明这并不能定义一个人的整个人生。

28.original: Sure, two are better than one, but they just cut off my arm.
28.b1: "Sure, two arms are better than one, but they only took my arm."
28.cn: “当然，两条总比一条好，但他们只是切掉了我的手臂。”
28.ai_notes: 'cut off': a phrasal verb meaning to remove a body part, usually through surgery.
28.remarks: 'cut off': 动词短语，指通过手术切除身体部位。

29.original: My spirit is one hundred percent intact.
29.ai_notes: 'intact': complete and not damaged; in one piece.
29.remarks: 'intact': 完整的，未受损的；完好无损。

31.original: Another amputee friend is an excellent golfer.
31.b1: Another friend who lost an arm is a great golfer.
31.cn: 我还有另一位失去手臂的朋友，他是一位出色的高尔夫球手。
31.ai_notes: 'amputee': a person who has had one or more limbs removed.
31.remarks: 'amputee': 截肢者，即失去一个或多个肢体的人。

32.original: One day I asked him how he had been able to develop such a near-perfect style with just one arm.
32.b1: I once asked him how he learned to play so well with only one arm.
32.cn: 有一次我问他，他是如何做到只用一只手臂就练就了如此完美的球技。

33.original: I mentioned that most golfers with two arms can't do nearly as well.
33.b1: I said that most people with two arms cannot play as well as he does.
33.cn: 我说大多数有双臂的人都打不出他这样的水平。
33.ai_notes: 'can't do nearly as well': cannot perform at a similar high level.
33.remarks: 'can't do nearly as well': 无法达到同样的高水平表现。

34.ai_notes: 'says a lot': means that the statement is very important or reveals a deep truth.
34.remarks: 'says a lot': 意味着该陈述非常重要或揭示了深刻的真理。

35.original: Well, it's my experience, he said, that the right attitude and one arm will beat the wrong attitude and two arms every time.
35.b1: He said that a good attitude and one arm are better than a bad attitude and two arms.
35.cn: 他说，好的心态加一只手臂，永远胜过坏的心态加两只手臂。
35.ai_notes: 'beat': to win against someone or something.

36.original: The right attitude and one arm will beat the wrong attitude and two arms every time.
36.b1: A good attitude and one arm will always beat a bad attitude and two arms.
36.cn: 好的心态加一只手臂，永远胜过坏的心态加两只手臂。
36.ai_notes: 'The right attitude': a positive mindset; the most important factor in overcoming challenges.
36.remarks: 'The right attitude': 正确的态度；克服挑战最重要的因素。

37.original: Think about that for a while.
37.b1: Think about that for a while.
37.ai_notes: 'Think about that for a while': a suggestion to reflect deeply on the meaning of what was just said.
37.remarks: 'Think about that for a while': 建议对刚才所说内容的含义进行深入思考。
